Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.input.action

Class FlixelActionSet

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.input.action.FlixelActionSet
All Implemented Interfaces:
FlixelDestroyable
public class FlixelActionSet extends Object implements FlixelDestroyable
Groups logical FlixelAction instances (digital and analog) and advances them on the same frame contract as FlixelInputManager. Actions read Flixel.keys, Flixel.mouse, Flixel.gamepads, and Gdx.input during update(float). This class is not an InputProcessor: you do not add it to InputMultiplexer. Framework keyboard and mouse processors stay the single libGDX entry points for those devices.

Lifecycle (normal games)

  1. Construct a subclass (or this type) after Flixel.initialize(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelGame).
  2. In the subclass constructor, create FlixelActionDigital / FlixelActionAnalog instances, call add(FlixelAction) for each, and configure FlixelInputBinding / FlixelAnalogAxisBinding.
  3. By default the set registers with FlixelActionSets; FlixelGame calls FlixelActionSets.updateAll(float) after Flixel.gamepads.update() and FlixelActionSets.endFrameAll() after keys, mouse, and gamepads endFrame() in render().
  4. From FlixelState.update(float) (or similar), read jump.justPressed(), move.getX(), etc.
  5. When the screen or mode ends, call destroy() so the set unregisters and clears members.

Example Usage


 public class PlayerControls extends FlixelActionSet {

   public final FlixelActionDigital jump;
   public final FlixelActionAnalog move;

   public PlayerControls() {
     jump = new FlixelActionDigital("jump");
     jump.addBinding(FlixelInputBinding.key(FlixelKey.SPACE));
     jump.addBinding(FlixelInputBinding.gamepadButton(0, FlixelGamepadInput.A));
     add(jump);

     move = new FlixelActionAnalog("move");
     move.addAxisBinding(FlixelAnalogAxisBinding.negXKey(FlixelKey.A));
     move.addAxisBinding(FlixelAnalogAxisBinding.posXKey(FlixelKey.D));
     move.addAxisBinding(FlixelAnalogAxisBinding.gamepadAxisX(0, FlixelGamepadInput.AXIS_LEFT_X));
     move.addAxisBinding(FlixelAnalogAxisBinding.gamepadAxisY(0, FlixelGamepadInput.AXIS_LEFT_Y));
     add(move);
   }
 }

Then, in a state for your game: 


 public class PlayState extends FlixelState {

   private final PlayerControls controls = new PlayerControls();

   @Override
   public void update(float elapsed) {
     if (controls.jump.justPressed()) {
       // fire jump once this frame
     }
     float mx = controls.move.getX();
     float my = controls.move.getY();
   }

   @Override
   public void destroy() {
     controls.destroy();
     super.destroy();
   }
 }

Steam Input

Set steamReader to an implementation that reads Steam Input API (for example via steamworks4j). Action FlixelAction.getName() strings should match your in-game actions manifest. Use FlixelSteamActionReaders.EMPTY when Steam is unavailable.

Tests

Pass false to FlixelActionSet(boolean) from an anonymous subclass so the set does not register globally, then call update(float) and endFrame() yourself.

    @Nullable FlixelSteamActionReader

  • Field Details

    • steamReader

      @Nullable public @Nullable FlixelSteamActionReader steamReader
      Optional Steam Input bridge; when non-null, digital and analog actions also read these values each update(float). Implement outside core with steamworks4j or similar.

  • Constructor Details

    • FlixelActionSet

      public FlixelActionSet()
      Registers this set with FlixelActionSets for automatic update(float) and endFrame().

  • Method Details

    • update

      public void update(float elapsed)
      Steps all member actions. Called from FlixelActionSets.updateAll(float) after gamepads update.
      Parameters:
      elapsed - Frame delta in seconds.

    • endFrame

      public void endFrame()
      Finalizes edge detection for digital and analog actions. Called from FlixelActionSets.endFrameAll() after keys, mouse, and gamepads endFrame().

    • destroy

      public void destroy()
      Description copied from interface: FlixelDestroyable
      Destroys the object and releases all resources.
      Specified by:
      destroy in interface FlixelDestroyable
